summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight drone attack with 154 strike UAVs targeting multiple Ukrainian regions, with air defenses shooting down or suppressing 138 targets. A Russian KAB strike on Kharkov killed a 23-year-old woman and wounded 10 people, while an FPV drone attack on a minibus in Zaporizhzhia killed two and injured six, including a child. President Zelensky stated Russia has again postponed its deadline to capture Donetsk Oblast, now set for December 31, marking approximately the 15th such deadline extension since the full-scale war began.
key_points: Ukraine's air defense shot down or suppressed 138 of 154 Russian strike drones during the night of June 30, with 13 hits recorded across 10 locations. (Source: @kpszsu/66892)
A Russian KAB aerial bomb struck Kharkov, killing a 23-year-old woman, wounding 10 people, damaging a tram, power grid, over 15 cars, and an enterprise building. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)
An FPV drone attacked a minibus in Zaporizhzhia, killing two people and wounding six, including a child. (Source: @UkraineNow/82257)

President Zelensky stated Russia has again postponed its deadline to capture Donetsk Oblast, now set for December 31, the approximately 15th such deadline set by Russia since the full-scale war began. (Source: @UkraineNow/82276)

Ukraine's Defense Forces struck three important bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk oblasts, targeting logistics routes used by the enemy for transferring personnel, weapons, ammunition, and equipment. (Source: @UkraineNow/82268)

Ukraine imposed scheduled power outages across all regions due to abnormal heat increasing energy consumption, with MP Kucherenko warning outages could increase to eight hours daily if the power grid is attacked. (Source: @UkraineNow/82272)

Putin stated he received new proposals to cease strikes deep into territory on both sides, and a proposal to limit combat actions to only four territories — Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, DNR, and LNR. (Source: @UkraineNow/82260)
TASS reported that up to 50 mobilized pre-retirement-age and limited-fitness individuals have been deployed to Sumy by Russian forces. (Source: @tass_agency/382997)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive overnight drone attack with 154 strike UAVs targeting multiple Ukrainian regions, with air defenses shooting down or suppressing 138 targets. A Russian KAB strike on Kharkov killed a 23-year-old woman and wounded 10 people, while an FPV drone attack on a minibus in Zaporizhzhia killed two and injured six, including a child. President Zelensky stated Russia has again postponed its deadline to capture Donetsk Oblast, now set for December 31, marking approximately the 15th such deadline extension since the full-scale war began.

points: Ukraine's air defense forces shot down or suppressed 138 of 154 Russian strike drones during the night of June 30, with 13 UAV hits recorded across 10 locations and debris falls at 2 additional locations. The attack included Shahed-type drones (including reactive variants), Gerbera, Italmas, and Parody-type decoy drones launched from Kursk, Orel, Donetsk, and Hvardiyske in Crimea. (Source: @kpszsu/66892)

Ukraine's Defense Forces struck three important bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk oblasts — a road bridge near Novoyazovsk in Donetsk Oblast and two railway bridges in Luhansk Oblast — targeting logistics infrastructure used by the enemy for transferring personnel, weapons, ammunition, and materiel. (Source: @UkraineNow/82268)

Scheduled power outages were imposed across all Ukrainian regions due to abnormal heat driving up energy consumption, with MP Kucherenko warning that outages could increase to eight hours daily if heatwaves coincide with attacks on the power grid, noting that domestic power generation in Kyiv has not been restored from past attacks. (Source: @UkraineNow/82272; @ukr_leaks_eng/31258)

President Zelensky stated in his evening address that Russia has again postponed its deadline to capture Donetsk Oblast, now set for December 31, noting this is approximately the 15th such deadline set by the Russian army since the start of the full-scale war. (Source: @UkraineNow/82276)
The death toll from the Dnipro attack rose to six, with the city's governor reporting damage to an enterprise, a school, private residences, and vehicles. (Source: @UkraineNow/82284)
Ukraine's Center for Countering Disinformation dismissed Putin's claim that Russian forces are '10 km from Sumy' as false. (Source: @UkraineNow/82277)

Russian forces launched 154 strike UAVs overnight, including Shahed-type drones (some reactive variants), Gerbera, Italmas, and Parody-type decoy drones, from directions including Kursk, Orel, Donetsk, and Hvardiyske in Crimea. (Source: @kpszsu/66892)

Russian Armed Forces struck Kharkov with a KAB aerial bomb, killing a 23-year-old woman, wounding 10 people, and damaging a tram, electrical network, over 15 vehicles, an enterprise building, and adjacent houses. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)
TASS reported that up to 50 mobilized pre-retition-age and limited-fitness individuals have been deployed to Sumy by Russian forces. (Source: @tass_agency/382997)
The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed that its 'North' military groupings pushed Ukrainian forces out of positions in Kharkov Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382993)
Moscow Mayor Sobyanin reported that air defense forces destroyed an additional 20 drones flying toward Moscow. (Source: @tass_agency/382995)

Gazprom Neft petrol stations in Moscow limited fuel sales to 30 liters of gasoline or 60 liters of diesel per customer at city stations, with fuel in canisters not being dispensed. (Source: @UkraineNow/82286)

Putin stated he received new proposals to cease strikes deep into territory on both sides, and a proposal to limit combat actions to only four territories — Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, DNR, and LNR — while claiming the cause is a 'catastrophic shortage of Ukrainian personnel.' (Source: @UkraineNow/82260)

A Russian KAB aerial bomb struck Kharkov, killing a 23-year-old woman and wounding 10 people, damaging a tram, power grid, over 15 cars, an enterprise building, and adjacent houses. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)
Russian Armed Forces struck targets in Kharkov, with a column of smoke rising after one of the explosions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31250)
The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed that its 'North' military groupings pushed Ukrainian forces out of positions in Kharkov Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382993)
Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31242)

Ukraine's Defense Forces struck a road bridge near Novoyazovsk in Donetsk Oblast and two railway bridges in Luhansk Oblast, targeting logistics routes used by the enemy for transferring personnel, weapons, ammunition, and equipment. (Source: @UkraineNow/82268)

President Zelensky stated Russia has again postponed its deadline to capture Donetsk Oblast, now set for December 31, the approximately 15th such deadline since the full-scale war began. (Source: @UkraineNow/82276)
Three UAV groups were reported moving from Dobropillya (Donetsk Oblast) toward Yuriivka along the inter-regional border. (Source: @eRadarrua/98734)
TASS reported that up to 50 mobilized pre-retirement-age and limited-fitness individuals have been deployed to Sumy by Russian forces. (Source: @tass_agency/382997)
Ukraine's Center for Countering Disinformation dismissed Putin's claim that Russian forces are '10 km from Sumy' as false. (Source: @UkraineNow/82277)
At least 3 UAVs were reported along the border toward Dobryanka in Chernihiv Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98707)
A reactive drone was reported from Sumy Oblast toward Chernihiv Oblast, course Talalaivka. (Source: @eRadarrua/98761)
Reactive UAVs were reported in the Pryluky district of Chernihiv Oblast heading in a southern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/66858)
A group of strike UAVs was reported in the northern part of Sumy Oblast on a variable course. (Source: @kpszsu/66882)
An FPV drone attacked a minibus in Zaporizhzhia, killing two people and wounding six, including a child. (Source: @UkraineNow/82257)
A Russian drone attacked the Zaporizhzhia regional administration building. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31252)
A reactive UAV was reported over Vilnyansk in Zaporizhzhia Oblast heading in a north-western direction. (Source: @kpszsu/66859)
Two strike UAVs were reported in Zaporizhzhia Oblast along the river toward Kuhugum. (Source: @eRadarrua/98748)
A reactive UAV was reported toward Bereznehuvate in Mykolaiv Oblast from the south. (Source: @kpszsu/66893)
A reactive UAV was reported toward Kryvyi Rih in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast from the east. (Source: @kpszsu/66876)
A reactive UAV was reported toward Sofiyivka in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/98762)

Ukraine's air defense shot down or suppressed 138 of 154 Russian strike drones during the night of June 30, with 13 hits recorded across 10 locations and debris falls at 2 additional locations. The attack included Shahed-type drones (including reactive variants), Gerbera, Italmas, and Parody-type decoy drones. (Source: @kpszsu/66892)
Moscow Mayor Sobyanin reported that air defense forces destroyed an additional 20 drones flying toward Moscow. (Source: @tass_agency/382995)
Multiple Shahed drones were reported heading toward Kremenchuk in Poltava Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68160)
Four Shahed drones were reported flying into Poltava. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68163)
A reactive Shahed was reported over Kropyvnytskyi railway station. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68180)
A reactive Shahed was reported heading toward Kropyivnytskyi from the south. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68179)
A reactive Shahed was reported on Dnipropetrovsk Oblast with course toward Kryvyi Rih/Kirovohrad Oblast. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68173)

key_developments: Russia launched 154 strike UAVs overnight from Kursk, Orel, Donetsk, and Crimea; Ukraine's air defense shot down or suppressed 138, with 13 hits across 10 locations. (Source: @kpszsu/66892)
A Russian KAB strike on Kharkov killed a 23-year-old woman, wounded 10 people, and damaged a tram, power grid, over 15 vehicles, and an enterprise building. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)
An FPV drone attacked a minibus in Zaporizhzhia, killing two people and wounding six, including a child. (Source: @UkraineNow/82257)

Ukraine struck three bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk oblasts — a road bridge near Novoyazovsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk — disrupting enemy logistics routes. (Source: @UkraineNow/82268)
President Zelensky stated Russia postponed its Donetsk Oblast capture deadline to December 31, the approximately 15th such deadline since the full-scale war began. (Source: @UkraineNow/82276)

Putin stated he received proposals to cease deep strikes on both sides and to limit combat to four territories — Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, DNR, and LNR — citing a claimed 'catastrophic shortage of Ukrainian personnel.' (Source: @UkraineNow/82260)
TASS reported up to 50 mobilized pre-retirement-age and limited-fitness individuals deployed to Sumy by Russian forces. (Source: @tass_agency/382997)
sections.threat_assessment: The large-scale overnight drone attack with 154 UAVs demonstrates Russia's continued capacity for sustained strategic strikes across multiple Ukrainian regions simultaneously. The deployment of reactive Shahed variants alongside decoy drones (Parody-type) indicates an evolving Russian UAV doctrine aimed at saturating Ukrainian air defenses. The KAB strike on Kharkov and FPV drone attack in Zaporizhzhia show persistent threats to civilian infrastructure and populations. Russia's reported deployment of limited-fitness mobilized personnel to Sumy suggests manpower pressures continuing to shape Russian operational decisions. The repeated postponement of the Donetsk Oblast capture deadline — now approximately the 15th extension — indicates persistent Russian difficulties in achieving stated operational objectives.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's air defense demonstrated significant capability by intercepting 138 of 154 incoming drones, though 13 hits across 10 locations indicate saturation challenges remain. The imposition of scheduled power outages across all regions reflects the compounding strain of extreme heat and prior infrastructure attacks on the energy system. Lukashenko's meeting with Xi Jinping, following two days at Putin's Valdai residence, signals continued diplomatic coordination among Russia's key partners, with China affirming readiness to assist Belarus 'within its capabilities.' The ISU's decision to allow Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete as neutral athletes from the 2026/27 season represents a further step toward international sporting normalization.
sections.intelligence_notes: The claim by TASS of 50 mobilized pre-retirement-age and limited-fitness individuals deployed to Sumy warrants monitoring as a potential indicator of deepening Russian manpower constraints. Putin's public articulation of ceasefire proposals — including limiting combat to four territories — may signal an effort to shape diplomatic narratives following reported discussions with Lukashenko. The Gazprom Neft fuel rationing in Moscow (30 liters per customer at city stations) suggests either supply chain disruptions or precautionary measures in response to Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure. The attack on Ukrainian oligarch Vadim Ermolaev in Monaco, as reported by Le Figaro and ukr_leaks_eng, represents a notable targeted incident outside the primary conflict zone.
